summary: Well-structured multi-layer test suite covering all three retention policies (max_age, max_total_size, keep_latest_n), CLI interface, error handling, and workflow structure. Unit tests thoroughly exercise policy combinations and interactions; end-to-end tests validate exact output against fixture cases with real GitHub Actions execution. Strengths: clear separation (unit/structural/e2e), exact output matching, good fixture organization, descriptive naming. Weaknesses: rigor gaps include no unit test for empty artifact list, missing edge cases (zero/negative sizes, keep_latest_n=0, identical timestamps, very small files), and some brittle string-based YAML validation. Would catch most regressions due to strong architectural layering and e2e coverage, but some edge-case bugs might slip through the unit tests.
